findings.title: Add Notification Priority Levels
findings.description: Implement a three-tier priority system for notifications to visually distinguish between urgent, normal, and low priority alerts.
assumptions: Users need to differentiate between critical and routine notifications.

evidence.excerpt: Users need a way to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.

recommendations: Design a visual representation for each priority level.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user receives notifications WHEN a notification is sent THEN it should display the correct priority level (urgent, normal, low).

findings.title: Fix Notification Delivery on Android 14
findings.description: Resolve the issue where push notifications fail to deliver on Android 14 devices, affecting approximately 8% of users.
assumptions: Users on Android 14 need reliable notification delivery.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate FCM token refresh and delivery callback issues.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user on Android 14 WHEN a notification is sent THEN it should be delivered successfully without manual intervention.

findings.title: Implement ML-based Notification Batching
findings.description: Develop a machine learning model to batch low-priority notifications into a digest format based on user engagement patterns.
assumptions: Users prefer receiving notifications in a digest format.

evidence.excerpt: Casual users prefer receiving non-urgent notifications in a digest format.

recommendations: Create a user engagement model to optimize notification timing.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user has low-priority notifications WHEN the ML model processes them THEN they should be batched and sent in a digest format at optimal times.

findings.title: Add User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Create a settings page that allows users to opt in/out of specific notification categories such as marketing, product updates, and social notifications.
assumptions: Users want control over their notification preferences.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Develop a new preferences API endpoint.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the notification settings page WHEN they select notification categories THEN their preferences should be saved and respected.

findings.title: Reduce Notification Delivery Latency
findings.description: Improve the notification delivery latency from the current 340ms to under 200ms to enhance user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Performance monitoring shows p95 notification delivery latency at 340ms.

recommendations: Implement asynchronous writes and batch inserts for analytics events.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a notification is sent WHEN it is delivered THEN the delivery latency should be less than 200ms.

findings.title: Conduct A/B Testing on Notification Formats
findings.description: Implement A/B testing for different notification formats to determine which format yields the highest click-through rate and lowest unsubscribe rate.
assumptions: Different notification formats may affect user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct A/B testing on different notification formats.

recommendations: Analyze results to optimize notification formats.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN different notification formats are deployed WHEN users interact with notifications THEN the CTR and unsubscribe rate should be measured and analyzed.

findings.title: Improve Accessibility of Notification Center
findings.description: Enhance the design of the notification center to address accessibility issues for users with screen readers and colorblindness.
assumptions: Accessibility is crucial for all users.

evidence.excerpt: Accessibility concerns have been raised regarding the notification center.

recommendations: Conduct accessibility testing and implement necessary design changes.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with accessibility needs WHEN they access the notification center THEN it should be navigable and usable with assistive technologies.

findings.title: Collect User Feedback on Notification Preferences
findings.description: Implement a mechanism to collect user feedback on notification preferences and experiences to inform future improvements.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for continuous improvement.

evidence.excerpt: There is a gap in collecting data on user preferences for notification timing and content.

recommendations: Create a feedback form accessible from the notification settings.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the feedback form WHEN they submit their preferences THEN the feedback should be recorded and analyzed.

findings.title: Monitor Notification Delivery Performance
findings.description: Set up monitoring and alerting for notification delivery performance metrics to ensure compliance with latency targets.
assumptions: Monitoring is essential for maintaining performance standards.

evidence.excerpt: The 95th Percentile Delivery Latency measures the time taken to deliver notifications.

recommendations: Implement dashboards for real-time monitoring of delivery metrics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place WHEN notification delivery metrics are collected THEN alerts should trigger if latency exceeds 200ms.

findings.title: Enhance Notification Engagement Metrics
findings.description: Develop additional metrics to track user engagement with notifications beyond CTR and unsubscribe rates.
assumptions: Comprehensive metrics will provide better insights into user behavior.

evidence.excerpt: There is a gap in collecting data on user preferences for notification timing and content.

recommendations: Identify and implement new engagement metrics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN new engagement metrics are defined WHEN they are implemented THEN they should accurately reflect user interaction with notifications.

summary: The analysis of customer insights and metrics findings has led to the creation of a comprehensive product backlog for SmartNotify. Key requirements include implementing notification priority levels, fixing delivery issues on Android 14, and enhancing accessibility. The backlog features a mix of critical, high, and medium priority items, ensuring a focus on user engagement and satisfaction while addressing technical performance and compliance needs.
